project.emptyHint: Click a project node above to view details

huoliGo.name: Vitality Go
techs: Built mobile H5 with Vue3 + Vite, unified state management via Pinia
Canvas-based activity trail and heatmap visualizations
Deep integration with Amap SDK for real-time location and route planning
Custom WebSocket heartbeat mechanism for stable real-time messaging
Unified request interceptor with Token refresh and graceful error fallback
huoliGo.thought: In health-focused products, data visualization is the key driver of user motivation — yet information overload can easily cause anxiety. Finding the balance between 'meaningful feedback' and 'lightweight experience' was the central design question I wrestled with. It also taught me that a front-end engineer's depth of product understanding directly shapes the quality of their component design.

fourHigh.name: Four-High One-Heavy
techs: Vue3 + Element Plus mid-to-back-end architecture with modular role-based routing
ECharts multi-dimensional health trend charts for BP, glucose, lipids, and more
JSON Schema-driven dynamic forms supporting conditional logic and multiple field types
Virtual scrolling optimization for tens-of-thousands patient lists — 60% faster first paint
RBAC model enforcing permission isolation across doctor, nurse, and admin roles
fourHigh.thought: In chronic disease management, doctors and patients have completely opposite information-density needs — doctors require dense dashboards while patients need ultra-simple guidance. Designing a component library that serves two radically different user mindsets simultaneously was the biggest challenge, and it gave me a much deeper appreciation of 'role-centered design'.

fuwaiWeight.name: Fuwai Weight Management
techs: Cross-platform development with uni-app, isomorphic across WeChat Mini Program and H5
Weight / BMI trend chart with milestone annotations and celebration animations
Meal-tracking module with calorie calculation and nutrient analysis logic
RESTful API integration with hospital HIS systems, handling complex data mapping
Doctor-patient messaging system supporting rich media and follow-up reminders
fuwaiWeight.thought: This hospital project made me realize that deep collaboration with back-end engineers and product managers matters more than any technology choice. The rigor of workflow design directly impacts real patient outcomes — even a single poorly worded button label could cause genuine medical misunderstanding. It reshaped my understanding of what 'responsible front-end development' truly means.

summaryHighlights.name: Summary — Project Highlights
techs: Abstracted a cross-project reusable component library with unified design language
Performance optimizations — virtual scrolling, lazy loading, code splitting — 40%+ faster first paint
Generic request layer encapsulating Token refresh, error-code mapping, and graceful fallback
RBAC-based multi-role permission system with menu- and button-level granularity
Dual-engine visualization strategy with ECharts + Canvas, balancing flexibility and render performance
Established front-end engineering standards: ESLint + Prettier + Husky for team-wide consistency
summaryHighlights.thought: Looking back across multiple projects, what truly drove my growth wasn't any single technology — it was the 'systems thinking' forged through tackling different business domains. How to design a front-end architecture that iterates quickly yet stays robust is the question I keep returning to. Every project's pain point is a refactoring opportunity, and every refactoring deepens my belief that simplicity is correctness.
